fetch_status: WebFetch firewalled (karpathy.github.io 403, bearblog 403). WebSearch returned extensive 2025 year-in-review summary text and microGPT details across 12 result pages.

atoms.text: LLMs are not evolved animals but summoned ghosts: entities optimized under entirely different constraints (text imitation, not survival) than biological intelligence; the animal lens is the wrong substrate for thinking about them.

RLVR (Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards) emerged in 2025 as the de facto new post-training stage; rewards are non-gameable (a math problem solves or it doesn't), unlike RLHF preference models that can be exploited.

Jagged intelligence: LLMs trained with RLVR spike in capability near verifiable domains (math, code) and crater elsewhere; the performance landscape is shaped by the distribution of verifier coverage, not by smooth generalization.

Ambient programming / vibe coding: in 2025 AI crossed a threshold so impressive programs are built simply in English with the source code becoming background substrate; programming exits its professional guild.
RLVR offered high capability/$ ratio, gobbling compute that was originally intended for pretraining; the training-stack mass-balance shifted away from pretraining toward verifier-bounded RL.
A full GPT (tokenizer, autograd, transformer, Adam, training loop, inference) fits in 200 lines of dependency-free Python; LLMs reduce to a small set of irreducible algorithmic components.
Reducing LLM implementations to bare essentials reveals the algorithm-level data_efficiency floor: complexity above 200 lines is library scaffolding, not conceptual content.

Verifiability thesis: AI is superhuman at code and fails at tasks like car-washing because the gradient of capability tracks the gradient of verifier-density; tasks with rich verifiers concentrate capability, tasks without verifiers stagnate.

Human neural nets are optimized for survival; LLM neural nets are optimized for imitating humanity's text. Per-bit supervision channels differ in cardinality and content; cross-channel comparison via 'intelligence' is a category error.

Continual learning emerged after online RL as the next stage in late 2025; Cursor's online-RL paper from < 3 months earlier was already superseded, signaling that the post-training stack is mutating faster than papers can age.

Six paradigm shifts in 2025: RLVR, ghost intelligence framing, vibe coding, jagged intelligence, online RL, nano banana (efficient model families) — the stack is undergoing concurrent re-architecting at multiple layers.

microGPT is the culmination of a decade of simplification (micrograd, makemore, nanogpt); the 200-line ceiling reflects an algorithmic compression limit that AI-coding-agent assistance cannot push lower without crossing into mere obfuscation.
